educational_objective: Support little chats with books that invite pointing, naming and what-next talk.
age_stage_nuance: Just past two, story time shifts from naming alone to short chats about what happens next and who did what.
what_to_look_for: Flaps, repeating lines, clear pictures, age overlap 18-48m, UK publisher primary.
what_to_avoid: Interest age from 3+ only, chapter length, Waterstones-only primaries.
methodology: Bench of UK chat-friendly board and picture books on Penguin, Bookshop and BookTrust; discarded interest-from-3 titles (Oi Frog, You Choose); ranked by talk prompts for 25-27m, ownership risk and buyability; URL-verified 2026-07-24. age-gate filter against 25-27 months.
buying_factor_memo: Talk prompts first (flaps, peepo, press). Then familiar UK staples with high borrow risk. Brand cap two per author/imprint across Top 10. Specialist ratings path after Amazon bot walls.

top_picks.ownership_note: Very high duplication risk; borrow or bring back out first.
top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop/BookTrust PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (Dear Zoo). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A sturdy lift the flap board book where each crate reveals a different animal to name, reject and send back to the zoo.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Just past two, they want to lead the page with a point and a word. Flaps that hide animals invite a short chat about too big, too fierce and just right. Start here when you want a familiar lift and talk game on the sofa.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #1: strongest flap talk pattern for this band; ages from 18 months.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop/BookTrust PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (Peepo!). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A classic peepo board book following a baby through the day with holes to peep and busy home scenes to name together.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Daytime scenes give more to talk about than a single animal flap. Peep holes invite them to spot who is in the room next. Good when Dear Zoo is already known and you want home life chat on the page.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #2: peepo spotting chat; different talk job from Dear Zoo.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop/BookTrust PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (Each Peach Pear Plum). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for I spy rhyme chat
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A rhyme and I spy picture book where storybook characters hide in busy scenes for pointing and naming together on the sofa.
top_picks.ember_verdict: I spy pages stretch chat beyond flap opens for just past two. They hunt for a face in the picture and tell you who they found today. Second Ahlberg slot when Peepo already covers hole peeping at home each day.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #3: I spy talk; second Ahlberg brand only.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK Hachette PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (Press Here). Prior Bookshop primary failed availability 403; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.age_mark_on_listing: Toddler interactive picture book; overlaps early years
top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for press and talk play
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: An interactive picture book that asks them to press, shake and tip dots so the page seems to answer with a new pattern.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Press Here turns reading into a shared doing game with short instructions they can follow on the sofa. The chat is about what happens next after each press. Good when flaps feel old and they want a page that answers back at story time.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #4: instruction talk different from flap/I spy staples.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop/BookTrust PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (Where's Spot?). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for spot the dog flaps
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A lift the flap search for Spot behind doors and lids, with animals saying Spot is not there until the last reveal.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Search flaps keep the chat on where is he and who is behind this door. Short no answers build to a yes ending they can shout. Useful when Dear Zoo flaps are known and they want a dog hunt instead.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #5: search talk; Eric Hill first slot.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop/BookTrust PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (The Very Hungry Caterpillar). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A classic food count story with holes through fruit pages and a caterpillar that eats through the week then turns into a butterfly.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Counting food through the week gives a clear what next chat without needing long text on the page. They can name the fruit and notice the holes together. Good when you want a short story arc they already half know from soft play visits.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #6: count and what-next arc; household staple.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop/BookTrust PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (That's Not My Puppy). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for touch and reject chat
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A touchy feely board book where each puppy is not quite right until the last soft page they can stroke and claim.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Touch patches invite a short reject and try again chat: not this one, try the next page. The ending soft patch is the yes they wait for. Good when flaps are sorted and they want texture talk on the page.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #7: touch reject talk; Usborne first slot.

top_picks.ownership_note: Only if Spot search is already a hit.
top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop/BookTrust PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (Spot Goes to the Farm). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A farm visit flap book where Spot looks for animals behind barn doors and hedges for a short search and name chat.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Farm flaps extend the Spot search into animal naming on a different setting at home. Second Eric Hill only when Where is Spot is already loved each week. Skip if one Spot book is enough for your shelf right now.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #8: farm search; second Eric Hill slot.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop/BookTrust PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (That's Not My Dinosaur). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A dinosaur themed touchy feely board book with textured patches to stroke while rejecting each wrong dinosaur page until the last soft yes.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Dinosaur textures keep the reject and claim chat fresh after the puppy version at home. Second Usborne touch book only for this shelf. Useful when they already know the That is Not My pattern and want a new creature to stroke.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #9: second Usborne touch title; different theme.

top_picks.evidence_notes: UK publisher/Bookshop/BookTrust PDP smoke-OK and buyable 2026-07-24 (Brown Bear, Brown Bear, What Do You See?). Amazon bot-walled in-agent; specialist exemption with long UK shelf presence.
top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for colour see and say
top_picks.product_name: Brown Bear, Brown Bear, What Do You See?
top_picks.brand: Bill Martin Jr and Eric Carle
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A repeating colour and animal see and say book with bold Eric Carle pictures and a line they can join in on.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Repeating lines let them join the chat before they can retell a full story alone. Colours and animals give easy points to name on each page. Good when you want a chorus style book after flap search titles on the sofa.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #10: join-in chorus talk; closes Top 10 with a different pattern.

skips.product_name: Oi Frog interest age from 3 years
skips.skip_reason: Interest age from 3 years does not overlap 25-27 months.
Interest age starts at 3 years; no overlap with 25-27 months.
Waterstones 403 to agents; use publisher or Bookshop primaries.

guidance_notes.title: Borrow first
guidance_notes.body: Dear Zoo, Spot, Peepo and Hungry Caterpillar turn up in many UK homes; ask before buying new.

ingestion_ready.notes: Repaired Best for prefix + methodology age step; re-run FF.
